1→/*
     2→** This file is part of eOn.
     3→**
     4→** SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
     5→**
     6→** Copyright (c) 2010--present, eOn Development Team
     7→** All rights reserved.
     8→**
     9→** Repo:
    10→** https://github.com/TheochemUI/eOn
    11→*/
    12→#pragma once
    13→#include "Parameters.h"
    14→#include "Potential.h"
    15→#include <string>
    16→#include <vector>
    17→
    18→/** @defgroup Jobs
    19→ *
    20→ * \brief ClientEON main procedures
    21→ *
    22→ * This page provides links to all of the available jobs that can be run by the
    23→ * ClientEON, as well as documentation on the job class, and the overview
    24→ * section relating the job structure to the rest of the program.
    25→ *
    26→ */
    27→
    28→/**
    29→ * @file
    30→ * @ingroup Jobs
    31→ *
    32→ * \brief The job class is used to serve as an abstract class for all jobs,
    33→ *  as well as to call a job at runtime based off of the passed in parameters.
    34→ *
    35→ * The Static members are used to tell at runtime which job to run as set by the
    36→ * parameters, and therefore as set by the config.init file. About half of the
    37→ * jobs are standalone, while others are run from routines with the same name. A
    38→ * certain subset of jobs do not run optimizers (SEE OVERVIEW) and are
    39→ * documented in their own files accordingly.
    40→ *
    41→ */
    42→
    43→/**
    44→ * Declaration of job class
    45→ */
    46→
    47→class Job {
    48→private:
    49→protected:
    50→  // make const
    51→  JobType jtype;
    52→  Parameters params;
    53→  std::shared_ptr<Potential> pot;
    54→
    55→public:
    56→  Job(std::unique_ptr<Parameters> parameters)
    57→      : jtype{parameters->main_options.job},
    58→        params{*std::move(parameters)},
    59→        pot{helper_functions::makePotential(params.potential_options.potential,
    60→                                            params)} {}
    61→  Job(std::shared_ptr<Potential> potPassed, const Parameters &parameters)
    62→      : jtype{parameters.main_options.job},
    63→        params{parameters},
    64→        pot{potPassed} {}
    65→  virtual ~Job() = default;
    66→  //! Virtual run; used solely for dynamic dispatch
    67→  virtual std::vector<std::string> run() = 0;
    68→  JobType getType() { return this->jtype; };
    69→};
    70→
    71→namespace helper_functions {
    72→std::unique_ptr<Job> makeJob(std::unique_ptr<Parameters> params);
    73→} // namespace helper_functions
    74→

    20→// This is a forward declaration of BondBoost to avoid a circular dependency.
    21→class BondBoost;
    22→
    23→#include <string>
    24→
    25→/* Data describing an atomic structure. This class has been devised to handle
    26→ * information about an atomic structure such as positions, velocities, masses,
    27→ * etc. It also allow to associate a forcefield for the structure through a
    28→ * pointer to function (potential()). The class can read and save data to a .con
    29→ * file (atom2con() and con2atom()). It can also save to a .xyz file
    30→ * (atom2xyz()).*/
    31→
    32→class Matter {
    33→public:
    34→  ~Matter() = default;
    35→  Matter(std::shared_ptr<Potential> pot, const Parameters &params)
    36→      : potential{pot},
    37→        usePeriodicBoundaries{true},
    38→        recomputePotential{true},
    39→        forceCalls{0},
    40→        parameters{&params},
    41→        nAtoms{0},
    42→        positions{Eigen::MatrixXd::Zero(0, 3)},
    43→        velocities{Eigen::MatrixXd::Zero(0, 3)},
    44→        forces{Eigen::MatrixXd::Zero(0, 3)},
    45→        biasForces{Eigen::MatrixXd::Zero(0, 3)},
    46→        biasPotential{nullptr},
    47→        masses{Eigen::VectorXd::Zero(0)},
    48→        atomicNrs{Eigen::VectorXi::Zero(0)},
    49→        isFixed{Eigen::VectorXi::Zero(0)},
    50→        cell{Eigen::Matrix3d::Zero()},
    51→        cellInverse{Eigen::Matrix3d::Zero()},
    52→        energyVariance{0.0},
    53→        potentialEnergy{0.0} {
    54→
    55→    m_log = spdlog::get("combi");
    56→  } // the number of atoms shall be set later
    57→    // using resize()
    58→  // Matter(Parameters *parameters,
    59→  //        long int nAtoms);      // prepare the object for use with nAtoms
    60→  //        atoms
    61→  Matter(const Matter &matter);                  // create a copy of matter
    62→  const Matter &operator=(const Matter &matter); // copy the matter object
    63→  // bool operator==(const Matter& matter); // true if differences in positions
    64→  // are below differenceDistance bool operator!=(const Matter& matter); //
    65→  // inverse of ==
    66→  bool compare(const Matter &matter, bool indistinguishable = false);
    67→
    68→  double
    69→  distanceTo(const Matter &matter); // the distance to the given matter object
    70→  double perAtomNorm(const Matter &matter); // the maximum distance between two
    71→                                            // atoms in the Matter objects
    72→  void
    73→  setPotential(std::shared_ptr<Potential> pot); // set potential function to use
    74→  std::shared_ptr<Potential> getPotential();    // get potential function to use
    75→  void resize(long int nAtoms);   // set or reset the number of atoms
    76→  long int numberOfAtoms() const; // return the number of atoms
    77→  Matrix3d getCell() const;
    78→  void setCell(const Matrix3d &newCell);
    79→  double getPosition(long int atom, int axis)
    80→      const; // return the position of an atom along one of the axis
    81→  void setPosition(
    82→      long int atom, int axis,
    83→      double position); // set the position of atom along axis to position
    84→  void setVelocity(
    85→      long int atom, int axis,
    86→      double velocity); // set the velocity of atom along axis to velocity
    87→  bool relax(bool quiet = false, bool writeMovie = false,
    88→             bool checkpoint = false, string prefixMovie = string(),
    89→             string prefixCheckpoint = string());
    90→
    91→  AtomMatrix pbc(const AtomMatrix &diff) const;
    92→  VectorXd pbcV(const VectorXd &diff) const;
    93→
    94→  size_t getPotentialCalls() const;
    95→  const AtomMatrix &getPositions() const; // return coordinates of atoms
    96→  AtomMatrix getPositionsCopy() const;    // return a modifiable copy
    97→  VectorXd getPositionsV() const;
    98→  AtomMatrix
    99→  getPositionsFree() const; // return coordinates of free atoms in array pos
   100→  VectorXd getPositionsFreeV() const;
   101→  void
   102→  setPositions(const AtomMatrix &pos); // update Matter with the new positions
   103→                                       // of the free atoms given in array pos
   104→  void setPositionsV(const VectorXd &pos);
   105→  void setPositionsFree(
   106→      const AtomMatrix &pos); // update Matter with the new positions of the
   107→                              // free atoms given in array pos
   108→  void setPositionsFreeV(const VectorXd &pos);
   109→
   110→  AtomMatrix getVelocities() const;
   111→  void setVelocities(const AtomMatrix &v);
   112→  void setBiasForces(const AtomMatrix &bf);
   113→  void setBiasPotential(BondBoost *bondBoost);
   114→  void setForces(const AtomMatrix &f);
   115→  AtomMatrix getAccelerations();
   116→
   117→  AtomMatrix getForces(); // return forces applied on all atoms in array force
   118→  AtomMatrix getBiasForces();
   119→  VectorXd getForcesV();
   120→  AtomMatrix getForcesFree();
   121→  VectorXd getForcesFreeV();
   122→
   123→  double getMass(long int atom) const; // return the mass of the atom specified
   124→  void setMass(long int atom, double mass); // set the mass of an atom
   125→  void setMasses(const VectorXd &massesIn); // set the mass of an atom
   126→  long getAtomicNr(
   127→      long int atom) const; // return the atomic number of the atom specified
   128→  void setAtomicNr(long int atom,
   129→                   long atomicNr);           // set the atomic number of an atom
   130→  VectorXi getAtomicNrs() const;             // Get the vector of atomic numbers
   131→  VectorXi getAtomicNrsFree() const;         // Get the vector of atomic numbers
   132→  void setAtomicNrs(const VectorXi &atmnrs); // set the vector of atomic numbers
   133→
   134→  int getFixed(long int atom)
   135→      const; // return true if the atom is fixed, false if it is movable
   136→  void setFixed(long int atom,
   137→                int isFixed); // set the atom to fixed (true) or movable (false)
   138→  // void setPotentialEnergy(double);
   139→  double getEnergyVariance();
   140→  // Eigen::VectorXd getForceVariance();
   141→  // double getMaxVariance();
   142→  double getPotentialEnergy();
   143→  double getKineticEnergy() const;
   144→  double getMechanicalEnergy(); // return the mechanical energy (i.e. kinetic
   145→                                // plus potential energy)
   146→
   147→  double distance(long index1, long index2)
   148→      const; // return the distance between two atoms in same configuration
   149→  double pdistance(long index1, long index2, int axis) const;
   150→  double distance(const Matter &matter, long index)
   151→      const; // the distance between the same atom in two cofigurations
   152→
   153→  long int
   154→  numberOfFreeAtoms() const; // return the number of free (or movable) atoms
   155→  long int numberOfFixedAtoms() const; // return the number of fixed atoms
   156→
   157→  long
   158→  getForceCalls() const; // return how many force calls that have been performed
   159→  void resetForceCalls(); // zeroing the value of force calls
   160→
   161→  double maxForce(void);
   162→
   163→  void writeTibble(std::string filename);
   164→  bool con2matter(std::string filename); // read con file into Matter, return
   165→                                         // true if successful
   166→  bool con2matter(FILE *file); // read con file and load data into Matter,
   167→                               // return true if successful
   168→  bool
   169→  convel2matter(std::string filename); // read con file with both coordinates
   170→                                       // and velocities into Matter
   171→  bool convel2matter(FILE *file); // read con file with both coordinates and
   172→                                  // velocities and load data into Matter
   173→  bool
   174→  matter2con(std::string filename,
   175→             bool append = false); // print con file from data in Class Matter
   176→  bool matter2con(FILE *file);     // print con file from data in Class Matter
   177→  bool
   178→  matter2convel(std::string filename); // print con file with both coordinates
   179→                                       // and velocities  in Class Matter
   180→  bool matter2convel(FILE *file); // print con file with both coordinates and
   181→                                  // velocities from data in Class Matter
   182→  void matter2xyz(std::string filename,
   183→                  bool append = false); // print xyz file from data in Matter
   184→  AtomMatrix getFree() const;
   185→  VectorXd getFreeV() const;
   186→  Eigen::Matrix<double, Eigen::Dynamic, 1> getMasses() const;
   187→
   188→private:
   189→  shared_ptr<spdlog::logger> m_log;
   190→  std::shared_ptr<Potential>
   191→      potential; // pointer to function calculating the energy and forces
   192→  bool usePeriodicBoundaries; // boolean telling periodic boundaries are used
   193→  mutable bool recomputePotential; // boolean indicating if the potential energy
   194→                                   // and forces need to be recalculated
   195→  mutable long
   196→      forceCalls; // keep track of how many force calls have been performed
   197→
   198→  // CON file header information, which is not used in the eon code
   199→  char headerCon1[512];
   200→  char headerCon2[512];
   201→  char headerCon4[512];
   202→  char headerCon5[512];
   203→  char headerCon6[512];
   204→
   205→  void computePotential();
   206→  void applyPeriodicBoundary();
   207→  void applyPeriodicBoundary(double &component, int axis);
   208→  void applyPeriodicBoundary(AtomMatrix &diff);
   209→
   210→  // Stuff which used to be in MatterPrivateData
   211→  const Parameters *parameters;
   212→  long nAtoms;
   213→  AtomMatrix positions;
   214→  AtomMatrix velocities;
   215→  AtomMatrix forces;
   216→  AtomMatrix biasForces;
   217→  BondBoost *biasPotential;
   218→  VectorXd masses;
   219→  VectorXi atomicNrs;
   220→  VectorXi isFixed; // array of bool, false for movable atom, true for fixed
   221→  Matrix3d cell;
   222→  Matrix3d cellInverse;
   223→  mutable double energyVariance;
   224→  mutable double potentialEnergy;
   225→};

   567→    )
   568→    """
   569→    Options:
   570→     - ``ams``: Amsterdam Modeling Suite potential.
   571→     - ``ams_io``: Amsterdam Modeling Suite via the I/O.
   572→     - ``ase_orca``: ASE interface for ORCA quantum chemistry package.
   573→     - ``bop``: Bond order potential for metals. [unused]
   574→     - ``bopfox``: Bond order potential, for metals. [unused]
   575→     - ``cuh2``: Potential for copper hydride systems.
   576→     - ``eam_al``: Embedded atom method parameterized for aluminum.
   577→     - ``edip``: Environment-Dependent Interatomic Potential, for carbon.
   578→     - ``emt``: Effective medium theory, for metals.
   579→     - ``ext``: External potential with system call interface.
   580→     - ``fehe``: Potential for iron-hydrogen systems.
   581→     - ``gpr``: Gaussian process regression potential.
   582→     - ``imd``: IMD simulation package interface.
   583→     - ``lammps``: The LAMMPS potentials.
   584→     - ``lenosky_si``: Lenosky potential, for silicon.
   585→     - ``lj``: Lennard-Jones potential in reduced units.
   586→     - ``ljcluster``: Lennard-Jones cluster potential.
   587→     - ``morse_pt``: Morse potential for platinum.
   588→     - ``mpi``: Communicate with an MPI process to calculate energy and forces.
   589→     - ``pyamff``: Python implementation of the AMFF potential.
   590→     - ``python``: Custom python potential.
   591→     - ``qsc``: Quantum Sutton-Chen potential, for FCC metals.
   592→     - ``spce``: Simple Point Charge model for water.
   593→     - ``sw_si``: Stillinger-Weber potential, for silicon.
   594→     - ``tersoff_si``: Tersoff pair potential with angular terms, for silicon.
   595→     - ``tip4p``: Point charge model for water.
   596→     - ``tip4p_h``: TIP4P model for water with hydrogen.
   597→     - ``tip4p_pt``: TIP4P model for water on platinum.
   598→     - ``unknown``: Placeholder for unknown potential type.
   599→     - ``vasp``: Vienna Ab-Initio Simulation Program (VASP) interface.
   600→     - ``xtb``: Extended Tight Binding model.
   601→    """
   602→    log_potential: Optional[bool] = Field(
   603→        default=None,
   604→        description="If true, write timing information about each force call to client.log.",
   605→    )
   606→
   607→    @validator("log_potential", always=True)
   608→    def set_log_potential(cls, v, values):
   609→        if v is None:

   611→            if potential in {"mpi", "vasp", "bop", "bopfox"}:
   612→                return True
   613→            else:
   614→                return False
   615→        return v

data.prompt:
Look for GSoC ideas pages or organization pages that Rohit Goswami has written before. Check these locations:
1. /home/rgoswami/Git/Gitlab/obsidian-notes/GSOC/ - list all files and read any that exist
2. Search for any existing GSoC-related content

Also fetch the python-gsoc ideas page format from https://python-gsoc.org/ to understand the expected structure for sub-org ideas pages. And look at:
- https://github.com/python-gsoc/python-gsoc.github.io to understand what PRs look like

     2→from ase.calculators.socketio import SocketClient
     3→import psutil
     4→import sys
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→
     7→run_path = Path.cwd() / "runs"  # Where things are run
     8→scratch_path = Path.cwd() / "nwchem_aux"  # scratch from NWChem
     9→nwchem_path = "/home/rgoswami/micromamba/envs/eongit/bin/nwchem"
    10→memory = "2 gb"  # Probably needs no change
    11→# Multiplicity of the molecule. 1 for singlets, 2 for doublets.
    12→mult = 1
    13→label = "bah"
    14→# run_dir = run_path / label
    15→scratch_dir = scratch_path / label
    16→nwchem_kwargs = dict(
    17→    label=label,
    18→    # directory=run_dir,
    19→    perm=scratch_dir,
    20→    scratch=scratch_dir,
    21→    command=f'mpirun -n {psutil.cpu_count(logical=False)} {nwchem_path} PREFIX.nwi > PREFIX.nwo',
    22→    # command=f'{nwchem_path} PREFIX.nwi > PREFIX.nwo',
    23→    memory=memory,

271-        Nf is calculated to be the number of processes in the rate
272-        table. Ns is calculated to be the number of searches that
273-        resulted in a process on the rate table.
274-
275:        When using recycling or kdb, it is useful to ignore processes
276-        that occur outside the hole, the region in which the last
277-        process took place.  Focusing on this region means you can do
278-        possibly far fewer searches to reach confidence. When using
279-        the hole to filter processes, Nf and Ns only take into account

    12→#pragma once
    13→#include "LowestEigenmode.h"
    14→#include "Matter.h"
    15→#include "Parameters.h"
    16→#include "Potential.h"
    17→
    18→// dimer method to find the lowest curvature mode
    19→class Dimer : public LowestEigenmode {
    20→
    21→public:
    22→  Dimer(std::shared_ptr<Matter> matter, const Parameters &params,
    23→        std::shared_ptr<Potential> pot);
    24→  ~Dimer() = default;
    25→
    26→  void initialize(Matter *matter, AtomMatrix); // initialize the dimer
    27→  void compute(std::shared_ptr<Matter> matter,
    28→               AtomMatrix initialDirection); // compute the lowest eigenmode
    29→  double getEigenvalue();                    // return the current eigenvalue
    30→  AtomMatrix getEigenvector();               // return the current eigenvector
    31→
    32→private:
    33→  std::shared_ptr<spdlog::logger> log;
    34→  std::shared_ptr<Matter> matterCenter; // center of the dimer
    35→  std::shared_ptr<Matter> matterDimer;  // one configuration of the dimer
    36→  AtomMatrix direction;                 // direction along the dimer
    37→  AtomMatrix rotationalPlane; // direction normal to the plane of dimer rotation
    38→  double eigenvalue;          // current curvature along the dimer
    39→  int nAtoms;
    40→
    41→  // The rotational plane that is going to be used is determined with the
    42→  // conjugate gradient method
    43→  void determineRotationalPlane(AtomMatrix rotationalForce,
    44→                                AtomMatrix &rotationalForceOld,
    45→                                AtomMatrix rotationalPlaneNormOld,
    46→                                double *lengthRotationalForceOld);
    47→
    48→  void
    49→  rotate(double rotationAngle); // rotate the dimer by rotationAngle (radians)
    50→  double calcRotationalForceReturnCurvature(
    51→      AtomMatrix &forceDiffOrthogonalToDimer); // determine the rotational force
    52→                                               // on the dimer
    53→};
    54→

    22→class MinModeSaddleSearch : public SaddleSearchMethod {
    23→
    24→public:
    25→  enum {
    26→    // DO NOT CHANGE THE ORDER OF THIS LIST
    27→    STATUS_GOOD,                           // 0
    28→    STATUS_INIT,                           // 1
    29→    STATUS_BAD_NO_CONVEX,                  // 2
    30→    STATUS_BAD_HIGH_ENERGY,                // 3
    31→    STATUS_BAD_MAX_CONCAVE_ITERATIONS,     // 4
    32→    STATUS_BAD_MAX_ITERATIONS,             // 5
    33→    STATUS_BAD_NOT_CONNECTED,              // 6
    34→    STATUS_BAD_PREFACTOR,                  // 7
    35→    STATUS_BAD_HIGH_BARRIER,               // 8
    36→    STATUS_BAD_MINIMA,                     // 9
    37→    STATUS_FAILED_PREFACTOR,               // 10
    38→    STATUS_POTENTIAL_FAILED,               // 11
    39→    STATUS_NONNEGATIVE_ABORT,              // 12
    40→    STATUS_NONLOCAL_ABORT,                 // 13

    94→            try:
    95→                queried = [int(q) for q in open(os.path.join(self.config.kdb_scratch_path, "queried"), 'r').readlines()]
    96→            except:
    97→                queried = []
    98→            if self.state.number not in queried:
    99→                queried.append(self.state.number)
   100→                f = open(os.path.join(self.config.kdb_scratch_path, "queried"), 'w')
   101→                for q in queried:
   102→                    f.write("%d\n" % q)
   103→                f.close()
   104→                kdb.query(self.state)
   105→
   106→        # If a per-state displacement atom list script was used, inject the
   107→        # cached result into the config so DisplacementManager's ListedAtoms
   108→        # picks it up.
   109→        if self.config.displace_atom_kmc_state_script:
   110→            from eon import _utils as utl
   111→            atom_list_str = str(self.state.info.get("Saddle Search", "displace_atom_list", ""))
   112→            if atom_list_str:
   113→                self.config.disp_listed_atoms = utl.parse_atom_list_str(atom_list_str)
   114→                # Ensure the listed-atom displacement method is active
   115→                if self.config.displace_listed_atom_weight == 0.0:
   116→                    self.config.displace_listed_atom_weight = 1.0
   117→
   118→        self.reactant = self.state.get_reactant()
   119→        self.displace = displace.DisplacementManager(self.reactant, moved_atoms, config = self.config)
   120→
   121→    def explore(self):
   122→        self.register_results()
   123→        if self.state.get_confidence(self.superbasin) < self.config.akmc_confidence:
   124→            self.make_jobs()
   125→        else:
   126→            num_cancelled = self.comm.cancel_state(self.state.number)
   127→            logger.info("Cancelled %i workunits from state %i",
   128→                        num_cancelled, self.state.number)
   129→            #XXX: Do we ever call explore on a completed state twice?
   130→            if self.config.kdb_on:
   131→                logger.info("Adding relevant processes to kinetic database")
   132→                for process_id in self.state.get_process_ids():
   133→                    output = kdb.insert(self.state, process_id)
   134→                    logger.debug("kdb insert: %s", output)
   135→
   136→    def generate_displacement(self):
   137→        if self.config.recycling_on and self.state.number != 0:
   138→            displacement, mode = self.recycler.make_suggestion()
   139→            if displacement:
   140→                self.nrecycled += 1
   141→                return displacement, mode, 'recycling'
   142→
   143→        if self.config.kdb_on:
   144→            displacement, mode = kdb.make_suggestion()
   145→            if displacement:
   146→                displacement.mass = self.reactant.mass
   147→                logger.info('Made a KDB suggestion')
   148→                return displacement, mode, 'kdb'
   149→
   150→        if self.config.saddle_method == 'dynamics':
   151→            return None, None, 'dynamics'
   152→
   153→        displacement, mode = self.displace.make_displacement()
   154→        return displacement, mode, 'random'
   155→
   156→
   157→class ClientMinModeExplorer(MinModeExplorer):
   158→    def __init__(self, states, previous_state, state, superbasin=None):
   159→        MinModeExplorer.__init__(self, states, previous_state, state, superbasin)
   160→        job_table_path = os.path.join(self.config.path_root, "jobs.tbl")
   161→        job_table_columns = [ 'state', 'wuid', 'type']
   162→        self.job_table = io.Table(job_table_path, job_table_columns)
   163→
   164→        if self.superbasin:
   165→            self.job_table.delete_row_func('state', lambda s: s not in self.superbasin.state_dict)
   166→        else:
   167→            self.job_table.delete_row_func('state', lambda s: s != state.number)
   168→

1→from pathlib import Path
     2→import sys
     3→import subprocess
     4→import tempfile
     5→import logging
     6→import typing as typ
     7→from dataclasses import dataclass
     8→
     9→from eon import fileio as eio
    10→from eon import config as econf
    11→
    12→
    13→@dataclass
    14→class ScriptConfig:
    15→    """Configuration for running an external atom list script."""
    16→
    17→    script_path: Path
    18→    scratch_path: Path
    19→    root_path: Path
    20→
    21→    def __post_init__(self):
    22→        """
    23→        Post-initialization processing to validate and resolve paths.
    24→        This method is automatically called by the dataclass constructor.
    25→        """
    26→        if not self.script_path.is_absolute():
    27→            self.script_path = self.root_path / self.script_path
    28→        if not self.scratch_path.is_absolute():
    29→            self.scratch_path = self.root_path / self.scratch_path
    30→
    31→    @classmethod
    32→    def from_eon_config(cls, config: econf.ConfigClass) -> typ.Self:
    33→        """
    34→        Factory method to create a ScriptConfig instance from the main EON config.
    35→        """
    36→        return cls(
    37→            script_path=Path(config.displace_atom_kmc_state_script),
    38→            scratch_path=Path(config.path_scratch),
    39→            root_path=Path(config.path_root),
    40→        )
    41→
    42→
    43→def normalize_atom_list_str(raw: str) -> str:
    44→    """Normalize a string of atom indices to comma-separated format.
    45→
    46→    Accepts space-separated, comma-separated, or mixed input.
    47→    Returns a canonical comma-separated string (e.g. "1, 3, 5").
    48→    """
    49→    # Split on commas and/or whitespace
    50→    import re
    51→    tokens = re.split(r'[,\s]+', raw.strip())
    52→    tokens = [t for t in tokens if t]
    53→    return ", ".join(tokens)
    54→
    55→
    56→def parse_atom_list_str(atom_list_str: str) -> list[int]:
    57→    """Parse a comma-separated atom list string into a list of ints."""
    58→    if not atom_list_str or not atom_list_str.strip():
    59→        return []
    60→    return [int(c.strip()) for c in atom_list_str.split(",") if c.strip()]
    61→
    62→
    63→def gen_ids_from_con(sconf: ScriptConfig, reactant, logger: logging.Logger):
    64→    if not sconf.script_path.is_file():
    65→        logger.error(f"displace_atom_list_script not found: {sconf.script_path}")
    66→        return ""
    67→
    68→    # Use a secure temporary file to pass the structure to the script
    69→    # The file is automatically deleted when the 'with' block is exited.
    70→    sconf.scratch_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    71→    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
    72→        mode="w+", delete=True, suffix=".con", dir=sconf.scratch_path
    73→    ) as tmpf:
    74→        # Save the displaced structure to the temporary file
    75→        eio.savecon(tmpf, reactant)
    76→        # Ensure all data is written to disk before the script reads it
    77→        tmpf.flush()
    78→
    79→        try:
    80→            # Execute the script, passing the temporary filename as an argument
    81→            proc = subprocess.run(
    82→                [sys.executable, str(sconf.script_path), tmpf.name],
    83→                capture_output=True,
    84→                text=True,
    85→                check=True,
    86→                cwd=sconf.root_path,
    87→            )
    88→            raw_output = proc.stdout.strip()
    89→            if not raw_output:
    90→                return ""
    91→            # Normalize to comma-separated format for config.py compatibility
    92→            return normalize_atom_list_str(raw_output)
    93→        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
    94→            logger.error(f"Error running displace_atom_list_script '{sconf.script_path}':")
    95→            logger.error(f"Stderr: {e.stderr.strip()}")
    96→            sys.exit(1)
    97→        except Exception as e:
    98→            logger.error(
    99→                f"An unexpected error occurred while running the displacement script: {e}"
   100→            )
   101→            sys.exit(1)
   102→

# Python GSoC Repository Structure

## Directory Organization

The repository contains year-based directories from 2017 through 2025, organizing content chronologically. Supporting directories include `css/`, `js/`, and `logos/` for styling and assets.

## File Format and Content

The site uses **HTML files** as its primary format. Key pages include:

- `index.html` - Main entry point
- `ideas.html` - Lists sub-organization project ideas
- `students.html` - Information for student applicants
- `mentors.html` - Mentor resources
- `deadlines.html` - Important dates
- `blogs.html` - Contributor blog posts
- `contributors.html` - Contributor recognition

The repository also contains `ApplicationTemplate.md`, indicating markdown support for certain content.

## Sub-Org Ideas Organization

According to the README, the site consolidates ideas from Python's sub-organizations. Rather than storing ideas directly in this repository, users are directed to "look at the issue trackers for our sub-orgs" and their individual websites. Each sub-org maintains its own getting started instructions and project tracking.

The site primarily serves as an **aggregator and informational hub** rather than a content storage system, with year-specific directories likely archiving historical GSoC information and selections.

1→/*
     2→** This file is part of eOn.
     3→**
     4→** SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
     5→**
     6→** Copyright (c) 2010--present, eOn Development Team
     7→** All rights reserved.
     8→**
     9→** Repo:
    10→** https://github.com/TheochemUI/eOn
    11→*/
    12→#pragma once
    13→
    14→#include "Eigen.h"
    15→#include "ObjectiveFunction.h"
    16→#include "Parameters.h"
    17→
    18→/** @defgroup Optimizers
    19→ *
    20→ * \brief ClientEON methods for optimizing atomic structures
    21→ *
    22→ * This page provides links to all the available optimizers that can be run by
    23→ * the ClientEON, as well as documentation on the optimizer class.
    24→ *
    25→ */
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * @file
    29→ * @ingroup Optimizers
    30→ *
    31→ * \brief The optimizer class is used to serve as an abstract class for all
    32→ * optimizers, as well as to call an optimizer at runtime based off of the
    33→ * passed in paramters.
    34→ *
    35→ * The set of optimizers are methods for optimizing atomic structures, solving
    36→ * unconstrained energy minimization. Only a certain set of job types that
    37→ * ClientEON runs can take advantage of the numeric optimizer (SEE OVERVIEW) and
    38→ * are documented in their own files accordingly.
    39→ *
    40→ */
    41→
    42→/**
    43→ * Declaration of the optimizer class
    44→ */
    45→
    46→class Optimizer {
    47→private:
    48→  const OptType m_otype;
    49→
    50→protected:
    51→  //! Parameters set by the config.init file
    52→  const Parameters &m_params;
    53→  //! An objective function relating a certain job method to the conjugate
    54→  //! gradient optimizer
    55→  std::shared_ptr<ObjectiveFunction> m_objf;
    56→
    57→public:
    58→  Optimizer(std::shared_ptr<ObjectiveFunction> a_objf,
    59→            const Parameters &a_params)
    60→      : m_otype{a_params.optimizer_options.method},
    61→        m_params{a_params},
    62→        m_objf{a_objf} {
    63→    SPDLOG_WARN("You should explicitly set an optimizer while constructing the "
    64→                "optimizer!!\n Defaulting to opt_method from the parameters");
    65→  }
    66→  Optimizer(std::shared_ptr<ObjectiveFunction> a_objf, OptType a_optype,
    67→            const Parameters &a_params)
    68→      : m_otype{a_optype},
    69→        m_params{a_params},
    70→        m_objf{a_objf} {}
    71→  //! optimizer deconstructor
    72→  virtual ~Optimizer() {};
    73→  //! Template for stepping the optimizer, returns convergence
    74→  virtual int step(double a_maxMove) = 0;
    75→  //! Template for running the optimizer; uses a series of steps, checking for
    76→  //! convergence each time
    77→  virtual int run(size_t a_maxIterations, double a_maxMove) = 0;
    78→};
    79→
    80→namespace helpers::create {
    81→std::unique_ptr<Optimizer> mkOptim(std::shared_ptr<ObjectiveFunction> a_objf,
    82→                                   OptType a_otype, const Parameters &a_params);
    83→}
    84→

1→/*
     2→** This file is part of eOn.
     3→**
     4→** SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
     5→**
     6→** Copyright (c) 2010--present, eOn Development Team
     7→** All rights reserved.
     8→**
     9→** Repo:
    10→** https://github.com/TheochemUI/eOn
    11→*/
    12→#pragma once
    13→#include "Eigen.h"
    14→#include "HelperFunctions.h"
    15→#include "Matter.h"
    16→#include "Optimizer.h"
    17→#include "Parameters.h"
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * @file
    21→ * @ingroup Optimizers
    22→ *
    23→ * \brief Direct optimization for energy minimization
    24→ *
    25→ * The conjugate gradient method is an algorithm for the numerical solution of
    26→ * particular stystems of linear equations, namely symetric,positive-definite
    27→ * ones.
    28→ *
    29→ */
    30→
    31→/**
    32→ * Decleration of the Conjugate Gradients optimizer
    33→ */
    34→
    35→class ConjugateGradients : public Optimizer {
    36→public:
    37→  //! Conjugate Gradients optimizer constructor
    38→  /*!
    39→   * \param std::shared_ptr<ObjectiveFunction> m_objf that tells the optimizer
    40→   * how to run \param const Parameters& m_params defined by the config.init
    41→   * file
    42→   */
    43→  ConjugateGradients(std::shared_ptr<ObjectiveFunction> a_objf,
    44→                     const Parameters &a_params)
    45→      : Optimizer(a_objf, OptType::CG, a_params),
    46→        m_directionOld{(a_objf->getPositions()).setZero()},
    47→        m_forceOld{(a_objf->getPositions()).setZero()}, // use setZero instead
    48→        m_cg_i{0} {
    49→    if (spdlog::get("cg")) {
    50→      m_log = spdlog::get("cg");
    51→    } else {
    52→      m_log = spdlog::basic_logger_mt("cg", "_cg.log", true);
    53→    }
    54→    m_log->set_pattern("[%l] [CG] %v");
    55→  }
    56→  //! Conjugant Gradient deconstructor
    57→  ~ConjugateGradients() = default;
    58→
    59→  //! Calls the next step in the algorithm
    60→  /**
    61→   * Either calls the single_step or line_search method depending on the
    62→   * parameters \return whether or not the algorithm has converged
    63→   */
    64→  int step(double a_maxMove) override;
    65→  //! Runs the conjugate gradient
    66→  /**
    67→   * \todo method should also return an error code and message if the algorithm
    68→   * errors out \return algorithm convergence
    69→   */
    70→  int run(size_t a_maxIterations, double a_maxMove) override;
    71→  //! Gets the direction of the next step
    72→  Eigen::VectorXd getStep();
    73→
    74→private:
    75→  //! Current step direction of the conjugate gradient
    76→  Eigen::VectorXd m_direction;
    77→  //! Algorithms previous step direction
    78→  Eigen::VectorXd m_directionOld;
    79→  //! Normalised version of the current direction vector
    80→  Eigen::VectorXd m_directionNorm;
    81→  //! Current force vector
    82→  Eigen::VectorXd m_force;
    83→  //! Previous force vector
    84→  Eigen::VectorXd m_forceOld;

     2→from array import array

     7→

    15→import numpy

    18→from eon.config import config as EON_CONFIG
    19→from eon.config import ConfigClass # Typing
    20→
    21→def tryint(s):
    22→    try:
    23→        return int(s)
    24→    except:
    25→        return s
    26→
    27→def alphanum_key(s):
    28→    """ Turn a string into a list of string and number chunks.
    29→        "z23a" -> ["z", 23, "a"]
    30→    """
    31→    return [ tryint(c) for c in re.split('([0-9]+)', s) ]
    32→
    33→def sort_nicely(l):
    34→    """ Sort the given list in the way that humans expect.
    35→    """
    36→    l.sort(key=alphanum_key)
    37→
    38→
    39→def get_communicator(config: ConfigClass = EON_CONFIG):
    40→    # This is an ugly hack to "remember" a communicator as it isn't possible to construct
    41→    # the MPI communicator multiple times and it needs to remember its object level variables.
    42→    if hasattr(get_communicator, 'comm'):
    43→        return get_communicator.comm
    44→
    45→    if config.comm_type=='cluster':
    46→        comm = Script(config.path_scratch, config.comm_job_bundle_size,
    47→                                   config.comm_script_name_prefix,
    48→                                   config.comm_script_path,
    49→                                   config.comm_script_queued_jobs_cmd,
    50→                                   config.comm_script_cancel_job_cmd,
    51→                                   config.comm_script_submit_job_cmd)
    52→    elif config.comm_type=='local':
    53→        comm = Local(config.path_scratch, config.comm_local_client,
    54→                                  config.comm_local_ncpus, config.comm_job_bundle_size)
    55→    elif config.comm_type=='mpi':
    56→        comm = MPI(config.path_scratch, config.comm_job_bundle_size)
    57→    else:
    58→        logger.error(str(config.comm_type)+" is an unknown communicator.")
    59→        raise ValueError()
    60→    get_communicator.comm = comm
    61→    return comm
    62→
    63→
    64→class NotImplementedError(Exception):
    65→    pass
    66→
    67→
    68→class CommunicatorError(Exception):
    69→    pass
    70→
    71→
    72→class EONClientError(Exception):
    73→    """An EON client finished without outputting results, it probably crashed."""
    74→    pass
    75→
    76→
    77→class Communicator:
    78→    def __init__(self, scratchpath, bundle_size=1, config: ConfigClass = EON_CONFIG):
    79→        self.config = config
    80→        if not os.path.isdir(scratchpath):
    81→            # should probably log this event
    82→            os.makedirs(scratchpath)
    83→        self.scratchpath = scratchpath
    84→        self.bundle_size = bundle_size
    85→
    86→    def submit_jobs(self, data, invariants):
    87→        '''Throws CommunicatorError if fails.'''
    88→        raise NotImplementedError()
    89→
    90→    def get_results(self, results):
    91→        '''Returns a list of dictionaries containing the results.'''
    92→        raise NotImplementedError()
    93→
    94→    def get_queue_size(self):
    95→        '''Returns the number of items waiting to run in the queue.'''
    96→        raise NotImplementedError()
    97→
    98→    def cancel_state(self, statenumber):
    99→        '''Returns the number of workunits that were canceled.'''
   100→        raise NotImplementedError()

1→---
     2→myst:
     3→  html_meta:
     4→    "description": "Guide to using the Kinetic Database (KDB) in eOn to store and reuse information about kinetic processes, speeding up aKMC simulations."
     5→    "keywords": "eOn Kinetic Database, KDB, aKMC acceleration, process recycling, tsase"
     6→---
     7→
     8→# Kinetic Database
     9→
    10→One of the bottlenecks in an aKMC simulation is performing the saddle point
    11→searches. The kinetic database has been demonstrated by
    12→{cite:t}`kdb-terrellDatabaseAtomisticReaction2012` to ameliorate this cost by
    13→storing information about processes as they are found and using it to predict
    14→future saddle points.
    15→
    16→In the following figure, the hydrogen of a carboxyl group on an Au(111) surface
    17→transfers to the other oxygen (a). In this process, the hydrogen is determined
    18→to be the only moving atom, and the two oxygen atoms to be its neighbors. The
    19→other atoms are stripped from the system and the resulting configurations are
    20→stored in the database (b). If in the future the system passes through a state
    21→with a local configuration closely resembling either minimum of (b), the kinetic
    22→database will suggest a saddle to converge with a dimer search. The dimer starts
    23→with this suggested configuration and mode, and if it is a good suggestion,
    24→converges very rapidly to the saddle.
    25→
    26→```{figure} ../fig/akmc-1.png
    27→---
    28→alt: Carboxyl group on an Au(111)
    29→class: full-width
    30→align: center
    31→---
    32→Snapshots of Carboxyl group on an Au(111). (a) Hydrogen of carboxyl transfers to another oxygen. (b) Other atoms are stripped and stored.
    33→```
    34→
    35→## Dependencies
    36→
    37→The kinetic database is contained in a library separate from `eOn`. It is part
    38→of the `tsase` python module [located here](http://theory.cm.utexas.edu/tsase).
    39→
    40→## Configuration
    41→
    42→```{code-block} ini
    43→[KDB]
    44→```
    45→
    46→```{eval-rst}
    47→.. autopydantic_model:: eon.schema.KDBConfig
    48→```
    49→
    50→## References
    51→
    52→```{bibliography}
    53→---
    54→style: alpha
    55→filter: docname in docnames
    56→labelprefix: KDB_
    57→keyprefix: kdb-
    58→---
    59→```
    60→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python
     2→
     3→# Assumes kdbinsert.py is in the path, that the user is inside an eon akmc
     4→# directory, and that the states directory is named 'states'.
     5→
     6→import os
     7→import sys
     8→
     9→args = ' '.join(sys.argv[1:])
    10→
    11→n = 0
    12→while os.path.exists(os.path.join('states', '%d' % n)):
    13→	lines = open(os.path.join('states', '%d' % n, 'processtable'), 'r').readlines()[1:]
    14→	procs = [line.split()[0] for line in lines]
    15→	for proc in procs:
    16→		path = os.path.join('states', '%d' % n, 'procdata')
    17→		reactant_path = os.path.join('states', '%d' % n, 'procdata', 'reactant_%s.con' % proc)
    18→		saddle_path = os.path.join('states', '%d' % n, 'procdata', 'saddle_%s.con' % proc)
    19→		product_path = os.path.join('states', '%d' % n, 'procdata', 'product_%s.con' % proc)
    20→		mode_path = os.path.join('states', '%d' % n, 'procdata', 'mode_%s.dat' % proc)
    21→		os.system('kdbinsert.py %s %s %s -o %s %s' % (reactant_path, saddle_path, product_path, mode_path, args))
    22→	n += 1
    23→

1→/*
     2→** This file is part of eOn.
     3→**
     4→** SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
     5→**
     6→** Copyright (c) 2010--present, eOn Development Team
     7→** All rights reserved.
     8→**
     9→** Repo:
    10→** https://github.com/TheochemUI/eOn
    11→*/
    12→#pragma once
    13→
    14→#include "HelperFunctions.h"
    15→#include "Job.h"
    16→#include "Matter.h"
    17→#include "MinModeSaddleSearch.h"
    18→#include "Parameters.h"
    19→
    20→/**
    21→ * @file
    22→ * @ingroup Jobs
    23→ *
    24→ * \brief Finds transition states by finding saddle points on the potential
    25→ * energy surface
    26→ *
    27→ * The saddle seach job implements a ref MinModeSaddleSearch, as well as an \ref
    28→ * Optimizer "optimizer". A saddle search is initiated by making a local
    29→ * displacement of atoms from their position at the minimum of the current
    30→ * state. It can either be run using the ref Dimer or the ref Lanczos min-mode
    31→ * method.
    32→ *
    33→ * The saddle seach job also optionally generates and works with a GP
    34→ * approximated potential energy system with the GPR Dimer minimum mode method
    35→ */
    36→
    37→/**
    38→ * Declaration of the Saddle Search job
    39→ */
    40→
    41→class SaddleSearchJob : public Job {
    42→public:
    43→  //! Saddle Search job constructor
    44→  /*!
    45→   * \param *params defined by the config.init file
    46→   */
    47→  SaddleSearchJob(std::unique_ptr<Parameters> parameters)
    48→      : Job(std::move(parameters)),
    49→        fCallsSaddle{0} {
    50→    log = spdlog::get("combi");
    51→  }
    52→  //! Saddle Search Job Deconstructor
    53→  ~SaddleSearchJob(void) = default;
    54→  //! Kicks off the Saddle Search
    55→  std::vector<std::string> run(void);
    56→
    57→private:
    58→  //! Runs the correct saddle search; also checks if the run was successful
    59→  int doSaddleSearch();
    60→  //! Logs the run status and makes sure the run was successful
    61→  void printEndState(int status);
    62→  //! Writes the results from the run to file
    63→  void saveData(int status);
    64→
    65→  //! Container for the results of the run
    66→  std::vector<std::string> returnFiles;
    67→
    68→  //! Initializes a ref MinModeSaddleSearch
    69→  std::unique_ptr<MinModeSaddleSearch> saddleSearch;
    70→  //! Initial configuration.
    71→  std::shared_ptr<Matter> initial;
    72→  //! Configuration used during the saddle point search.
    73→  std::shared_ptr<Matter> saddle;
    74→  //! Configuration used during the saddle point search.
    75→  std::shared_ptr<Matter> displacement;
    76→
    77→  //! Force calls to find the saddle
    78→  int fCallsSaddle;
    79→
    80→  std::shared_ptr<spdlog::logger> log;
    81→};
    82→

1→
     2→'''
     3→Con(figuration) i/o library
     4→'''
     5→import configparser
     6→#from io import BytesIO as StringIO
     7→from io import StringIO
     8→import logging
     9→logger = logging.getLogger('io')
    10→import numpy
    11→import os
    12→
    13→import pickle as pickle
    14→
    15→from eon import atoms
    16→from eon.config import config
    17→
    18→def save_prng_state():
    19→    state = numpy.random.get_state()
    20→    fh = open('prng.pkl', 'wb')
    21→    pickle.dump(state, fh, pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL)
    22→
    23→def get_prng_state():
    24→    fh = open('prng.pkl', 'rb')
    25→    state = pickle.load(fh)
    26→    numpy.random.set_state(state)
    27→
    28→def length_angle_to_box(boxlengths, angles):
    29→    box = numpy.zeros( (3,3) )
    30→    angles *= numpy.pi/180.0
    31→    box[0][0] = 1.0
    32→    box[1][0] = numpy.cos(angles[0])
    33→    box[1][1] = numpy.sin(angles[0])
    34→    box[2][0] = numpy.cos(angles[1])
    35→    box[2][1] = (numpy.cos(angles[2])-box[1][0]*box[2][0])/box[1][1]
    36→    box[2][2] = numpy.sqrt(1.0-box[2][0]**2-box[2][1]**2)
    37→    box[0,:]*=boxlengths[0]
    38→    box[1,:]*=boxlengths[1]
    39→    box[2,:]*=boxlengths[2]
    40→    return box
    41→
    42→def box_to_length_angle(box):
    43→    lengths = numpy.zeros(3)
    44→    lengths[0] = numpy.linalg.norm(box[0,:])
    45→    lengths[1] = numpy.linalg.norm(box[1,:])
    46→    lengths[2] = numpy.linalg.norm(box[2,:])
    47→    angles = numpy.zeros(3)
    48→    angles[0] = numpy.arccos(numpy.dot(box[0,:]/lengths[0],box[1,:]/lengths[1]))
    49→    angles[1] = numpy.arccos(numpy.dot(box[0,:]/lengths[0],box[2,:]/lengths[2]))
    50→    angles[2] = numpy.arccos(numpy.dot(box[1,:]/lengths[1],box[2,:]/lengths[2]))
    51→    angles *= 180.0/numpy.pi
    52→    return lengths, angles
    53→
    54→
    55→def loadcons(filename):
    56→    filein = open(filename, 'r')
    57→    p = []
    58→    while True:
    59→        try:
    60→            p.append(loadcon(filein, reset=False))
    61→        except:
    62→            return p
    63→
    64→
    65→def loadposcars(filename):
    66→    filein = open(filename, 'r')
    67→    p = []
    68→    while True:
    69→        try:
    70→            p.append(loadposcar(filein))
    71→        except:
    72→            return p
    73→
    74→
    75→def loadcon(filein, reset = True):
    76→    '''
    77→    Load a con file
    78→        filein: may be either a filename or a file-like object
    79→    '''
    80→    if hasattr(filein, 'readline'):
    81→        con = filein
    82→    else:
    83→        con = open(filein, 'r')
    84→    con.readline() # line 1: comment
    85→    con.readline() # line 2: comment
    86→    # determine how many dimensions
    87→    tmp = numpy.array(con.readline().split()) # line 3: Box lengths
    88→    for i in range(len(tmp)):
    89→        dim=i+1
    90→        try: float(tmp[i])
    91→        except:
    92→            dim=i
    93→            break
    94→    # handle the box
    95→    boxlengths=numpy.zeros(dim)
    96→    for i in range(dim):
    97→        boxlengths[i]=float(tmp[i])
    98→    boxangles=numpy.array([ float(f) for f in con.readline().split()[0:dim] ]) # line 4: Box angles
    99→    boxtemp=numpy.zeros((dim,dim),'d')
   100→    boxtemp = length_angle_to_box(boxlengths,boxangles)

1→#!/usr/bin/env python
     2→
     3→import os
     4→import sys
     5→sys.path.insert(0, "../../")
     6→from ndiff import ndiff
     7→
     8→test_path = os.path.split(os.path.realpath(__file__))[0]
     9→test_name = os.path.basename(test_path)
    10→
    11→os.system("../../../akmc.py --reset --force --quiet")
    12→for i in range(15):
    13→    retval = os.system("../../../akmc.py --quiet")
    14→    if retval:
    15→        print("%s: problem running eon.py" % test_name)
    16→        sys.exit(1)
    17→
    18→same, max_rel_err, reason = ndiff("dynamics.test", "dynamics.txt", 0.01)
    19→if same:
    20→    print("%s: passed maximum relative error of %.3e"%(test_name,max_rel_err))
    21→    os.system("../../../akmc.py --reset --force --quiet")
    22→    sys.exit(0)
    23→else:
    24→    print("%s: failed %s"%(test_name,reason))
    25→    sys.exit(1)
    26→

    21→        return
    22→    logger.debug("KDB inserting process")
    23→    reactant = aselite.read_any(state.proc_reactant_path(process_id))
    24→    saddle   = aselite.read_any(state.proc_saddle_path(process_id))
    25→    product  = aselite.read_any(state.proc_product_path(process_id))
    26→    mode     = state.get_process_mode(process_id)
    27→    db = local_db.LocalDB(config.kdb_name)
    28→    params = db.get_params()
    29→    insert_sub_class = local_insert.LocalInsert()
    30→    insert_sub_class.insert(reactant, saddle, product, mode=mode, nf=params['nf'],
    31→               dc=params['dc'], mac=params['mac'], kdbname=config.kdb_name)
    32→
    33→def query(state):
    34→    try:
    35→        from kdb import aselite
    36→        from kdb import local_db
    37→        from kdb import local_query
    38→    except:
    39→        logger.error('Python module kdb not found, kdb will not be used.')
    40→        return
    41→    if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches")):
    42→        shutil.rmtree(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches"))
    43→    os.makedirs(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches"))
    44→    kdbpath = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(config.path_root, config.kdb_path))
    45→    if state.number == 0:
    46→        reactant = aselite.read_any(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(config.path_root, "pos.con")))
    47→    else:
    48→        reactant = aselite.read_any(os.path.abspath(state.reactant_path))
    49→    db = local_db.LocalDB(config.kdb_name)
    50→    params = db.get_params()
    51→    query_sub_class = local_query.LocalQuery()
    52→    query_sub_class.query(reactant, os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches"),
    53→                          nodupes = config.kdb_nodupes, kdbname=config.kdb_name,
    54→                          dc=params['dc'], nf=params['nf'])
    55→
    56→def make_suggestion():
    57→    if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches")):
    58→        dones = glob.glob(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches",".done_*"))
    59→        if len(dones) > 0:
    60→            number = dones[0].split("_")[1]
    61→            try:
    62→                displacement = io.loadcon(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches", "SADDLE_%s" % number))
    63→            except FloatingPointError:
    64→                displacement = io.loadposcar(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches", "SADDLE_%s" % number))
    65→            except ValueError:
    66→                displacement = io.loadposcar(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches", "SADDLE_%s" % number))
    67→            mode = [[float(i) for i in l.strip().split()] for l in
    68→                    open(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches",
    69→                    "MODE_%s" % number), 'r').readlines()[:]]
    70→            os.remove(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches", ".done_%s" % number))
    71→            os.remove(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches", "SADDLE_%s" % number))
    72→            os.remove(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches", "MODE_%s" % number))
    73→            return displacement, mode
    74→    return None, None
    75→

## Research Summary: ASE Usage in eOn Server

Based on a thorough search of the eOn codebase, here are my findings about how ASE (Atomic Simulation Environment) is used:

### 1. **ASE IMPORTS AND PROPER DEPENDENCY USAGE**

The eOn codebase has several places where ASE is used as a proper external dependency:

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/client/potentials/ASE_NWCHEM/server.py` (Lines 1-8)**
```python
from ase.calculators.nwchem import NWChem
from ase.calculators.socketio import SocketIOCalculator
from ase.build import molecule
```

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/client/potentials/ASE_NWCHEM/client.py` (Lines 1-8)**
```python
from ase.calculators.nwchem import NWChem
from ase.calculators.socketio import SocketClient
from ase.build import molecule
```

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/eon_kdb.py` (Lines 16, 35)**
```python
from kdb import aselite
# Uses aselite.read_any() for reading atomic structures (lines 23-25, 46, 48)
```

Additional ASE usage in client potentials:
- `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/client/potentials/SocketNWChem/design/ase_nwchem_caller.py` - uses `ase.io`
- `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/client/potentials/SocketNWChem/design/fancy_wrapper.py` - uses `ase.units`
- `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/client/potentials/ZBL/wip_explorations/generate_structures.py` - uses `ase.Atoms` and `ase.io.write`

### 2. **OLDER/HARDCODED ASE-LIKE IMPLEMENTATION**

The most significant finding is that eOn has its **own custom `Atoms` class** that reimplements core ASE-like functionality:

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/atoms.py` (894 lines)**

This is a complete custom implementation that duplicates ASE's Atoms object:

```python
# Lines 10-31: Core Atoms class definition
class Atoms:
    """ The Atoms class. """
    
    def __init__(self, n_atoms):
        self.r = numpy.zeros((n_atoms,3))           # positions
        self.free = numpy.ones(n_atoms)             # free/frozen flags
        self.box = numpy.zeros((3,3))               # unit cell
        self.names = ['']*n_atoms                   # atom symbols
        self.mass = numpy.zeros(n_atoms)            # atomic masses
    
    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.r)
    
    def copy(self):
        p = Atoms(len(self))
        p.r = self.r.copy()
        # ... copies all attributes
```

**Key differences from ASE:**
- Stores atomic positions in `.r` (custom)
- Uses `.free` array for frozen/mobile atom flags (eOn-specific)
- Uses `.names` list (ASE uses `.numbers` or `.symbols`)
- Uses `.mass` array separate from element info (ASE integrates this)

**Why this exists:** The custom Atoms class predates or was designed independently from ASE integration, and eOn uses its own lightweight structure representation for compatibility with its historical data formats (`.con` files).

### 3. **FILE I/O AND CON FORMAT**

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/fileio.py` (760 lines)**

This module handles `.con` file I/O - eOn's native atomic structure format:

**Key functions:**
- `loadcon(filein, reset=True)` - Lines 75-131: Reads `.con` files into custom `atoms.Atoms` objects
- `savecon(fileout, p, w='w')` - Lines 133-174: Writes `atoms.Atoms` objects to `.con` files
- `loadposcar(filein)` - Lines 264-321: Reads VASP POSCAR files into `atoms.Atoms`
- `saveposcar(fileout, p, w='w', direct=False)` - Lines 324-363: Writes `atoms.Atoms` to POSCAR format

**Con file format structure (lines 84-131):**
```
Line 1-2:   Comments
Line 3:     Box lengths
Line 4:     Box angles
Line 5-6:   Comments
Line 7:     Number of atom types
Line 8:     Number of atoms per type
Line 9:     Mass of each type
Lines 10+:  Atom positions and mobility flags
```

### 4. **CONFIGURATION AND POTENTIAL DEFINITIONS**

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/schema.py` (Lines 529-530, 572)**

ASE potentials are defined in the schema:
```python
potential: Literal[
    "ase_nwcem",     # ASE with NWChem (quantum)
    "ase_orca",      # ASE with ORCA (quantum)
    # ... other 40+ potentials
] = Field(default="lj", description="Type of potential to execute.")
```

**Documented options (lines 572):**
- `ase_orca`: ASE interface for ORCA quantum chemistry package
- `ase_nwcem`: ASE with NWChem quantum package

**Associated configuration classes (lines 710-742):**
- `ASE_NWCHEM` class (lines 710-729)
- `ASE_ORCA` class (lines 731-742)

### 5. **KDB INTEGRATION (ASE-DERIVED)**

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/eon_kdb.py`**

The Kinetic Database integration uses `aselite` (a lightweight ASE-like module):

**Usage pattern (lines 14-31):**
```python
def insert(state, process_id):
    try:
        from kdb import aselite          # External ASE-like module
        from kdb import local_db
        from kdb import local_insert
    except:
        logger.error('Python module kdb not found')
        return
    
    # Lines 23-25: Convert .con files to ASE-like objects
    reactant = aselite.read_any(state.proc_reactant_path(process_id))
    saddle   = aselite.read_any(state.proc_saddle_path(process_id))
    product  = aselite.read_any(state.proc_product_path(process_id))
```

This shows that `aselite` is an optional external module that provides ASE-like atom reading capabilities without depending on the full ASE package.

### 6. **SUMMARY TABLE**

| Aspect | Details | File(s) |
|--------|---------|---------|
| **Native Atoms Class** | Custom lightweight `Atoms` implementation (894 lines) | `/eon/atoms.py` |
| **Con File I/O** | Native `.con` format reader/writer (760 lines) | `/eon/fileio.py` |
| **ASE Direct Usage** | NWChem & ORCA calculators via ASE | `/client/potentials/ASE_NWCHEM/` |
| **ASE-like Module** | `aselite` from external kdb package | `/eon/eon_kdb.py` lines 16, 35 |
| **Configuration** | `ase_nwcem`, `ase_orca` potential types | `/eon/schema.py` lines 529-530, 572 |
| **Element Data** | Hardcoded periodic table (119 elements) | `/eon/atoms.py` lines 774-894 |

### 7. **KEY CONCLUSIONS**

1. **Dual Architecture:** eOn maintains its own minimal Atoms/structure class separate from ASE, despite having optional ASE integration
2. **ASE is Optional:** ASE usage is limited to specific quantum chemistry calculators (NWChem, ORCA) and the optional kinetic database module
3. **Legacy Data Format:** The `.con` file format and custom Atoms class are core to eOn's legacy infrastructure
4. **Lightweight Approach:** Rather than depend heavily on ASE, eOn uses a lightweight `aselite` module for compatibility
5. **No ASE Direct Imports in Server Code:** The main eOn server code (`/eon/*.py`) does not directly import ASE—it only uses the custom Atoms class and fileio module

| **ASE-like Module** | `aselite` from external kdb package | `/eon/eon_kdb.py` lines 16, 35 |
| **Configuration** | `ase_nwcem`, `ase_orca` potential types | `/eon/schema.py` lines 529-530, 572 |
| **Element Data** | Hardcoded periodic table (119 elements) | `/eon/atoms.py` lines 774-894 |

### 7. **KEY CONCLUSIONS**

1. **Dual Architecture:** eOn maintains its own minimal Atoms/structure class separate from ASE, despite having optional ASE integration
2. **ASE is Optional:** ASE usage is limited to specific quantum chemistry calculators (NWChem, ORCA) and the optional kinetic database module
3. **Legacy Data Format:** The `.con` file format and custom Atoms class are core to eOn's legacy infrastructure
4. **Lightweight Approach:** Rather than depend heavily on ASE, eOn uses a lightweight `aselite` module for compatibility
5. **No ASE Direct Imports in Server Code:** The main eOn server code (`/eon/*.py`) does not directly import ASE—it only uses the custom Atoms class and fileio module

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive research report.

## Kinetic Database (KDB) Implementation Research Report

### Overview
The Kinetic Database (KDB) is an optional feature in eOn that accelerates aKMC simulations by storing information about saddle points and reusing them when similar local configurations are encountered. It's implemented as a wrapper around an external Python package.

---

### 1. KDB-Related Files

#### A. Main KDB Implementation
**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/eon_kdb.py`** (74 lines)
- **Lines 14-31**: `insert(state, process_id)` function
  - Imports external `kdb` module components: `aselite`, `local_db`, `local_insert`
  - Uses `LocalDB(config.kdb_name)` to open database
  - Calls `db.get_params()` to retrieve parameters (nf, dc, mac)
  - Inserts reactant, saddle, product structures and displacement modes

- **Lines 33-54**: `query(state)` function
  - Imports external `kdb` module: `aselite`, `local_db`, `local_query`
  - Creates scratch directories for KDB matches at `config.kdb_scratch_path/kdbmatches`
  - Reads reactant structure from initial state or previous state
  - Calls `LocalQuery().query()` to search database
  - Stores results in temporary directory with specific naming convention

- **Lines 56-74**: `make_suggestion()` function
  - Reads `.done_*` files from kdbmatches directory
  - Extracts displacement configuration from `SADDLE_*` and mode from `MODE_*` files
  - Returns displacement/mode pair or `(None, None)`
  - Performs cleanup by deleting temporary files after reading

#### B. Configuration Files
**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/config.yaml`** (Lines 890-918)
```yaml
KDB:
  options:
    use_kdb: boolean (default: False)
    remove_duplicates: boolean (default: False)
    kdb_only: boolean (default: False)
    kdb_name: string (default: kdb.db)
    kdb_nf: float (default: 0.2)
    kdb_dc: float (default: 0.3)
    kdb_mac: float (default: 0.7)
```

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/config.py`** (Lines 277-286)
```python
# KDB section
self.kdb_on = parser.getboolean('KDB', 'use_kdb')
self.kdb_only = parser.getboolean('KDB', 'kdb_only')
self.kdb_scratch_path = parser.get('Paths', 'kdb_scratch')
self.kdb_path = parser.get('Paths', 'kdb')
self.kdb_nodupes = parser.getboolean('KDB', 'remove_duplicates')
self.kdb_name = parser.get('KDB', 'kdb_name')
self.kdb_nf = parser.get('KDB', 'kdb_nf')
self.kdb_dc = parser.get('KDB', 'kdb_dc')
self.kdb_mac = parser.get('KDB', 'kdb_mac')
```

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/schema.py`** (Lines 1032-1048)
```python
class KDBConfig(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(use_attribute_docstrings=True)
    
    use_kdb: bool = False
    kdb_only: bool = Field(
        default=False,
        description="Only use KDB in an AKMC simulation, no random searches.",
    )
    remove_duplicates: bool = Field(
        default=False,
        description="KDB will not make duplicate suggestions. This can slow KDB querying, so it may be best to use this only for slow potentials (DFT, etc.).",
    )
    kdb_name: str = "kdb.db"
    # TODO(rg): These are in config.yaml, not sure what they do..
    kdb_nf: float = 0.2
    kdb_dc: float = 0.3
    kdb_mac: float = 0.7
```

---

### 2. Storage & Data Format

#### Storage Backend
- **Type**: File-based with external database format
- **Database File**: `kdb.db` (default name, configurable via `config.kdb_name`)
- **Location**: `config.kdb_path` (default: `%(main_directory)s/kdb/`)
- **Scratch/Cache**: `config.kdb_scratch_path` (default: `%(main_directory)s/kdbscratch/`)

#### Data Structures
The KDB stores:
1. **Reactant structures** - minimum energy configurations
2. **Saddle point structures** - transition state geometries
3. **Product structures** - final stable configurations
4. **Displacement modes** - eigenvector directions for saddle searches
5. **Local environment information** - neighbor relationships

#### File Format in Scratch Directory
- **Temporary matches**: `kdbmatches/SADDLE_<number>` - structure files
- **Mode information**: `kdbmatches/MODE_<number>` - displacement mode vectors
- **Tracking file**: `kdbmatches/.done_<number>` - marks completed matches
- **Query tracking**: `kdb_scratch_path/queried` - list of queried state numbers

---

### 3. Integration Points in eOn

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/explorer.py`**

- **Lines 17-19**: Import statement
  ```python
  #import kdb
  from eon import eon_kdb as kdb
  ```

- **Lines 91-104**: Query on state initialization
  ```python
  if self.config.kdb_on:
      if not os.path.isdir(self.config.kdb_scratch_path):
          os.makedirs(self.config.kdb_scratch_path)
      try:
          queried = [int(q) for q in open(os.path.join(self.config.kdb_scratch_path, "queried"), 'r').readlines()]
      except:
          queried = []
      if self.state.number not in queried:
          queried.append(self.state.number)
          f = open(os.path.join(self.config.kdb_scratch_path, "queried"), 'w')
          for q in queried:
              f.write("%d\n" % q)
          f.close()
          kdb.query(self.state)
  ```

- **Lines 130-134**: Insert on state completion
  ```python
  if self.config.kdb_on:
      logger.info("Adding relevant processes to kinetic database")
      for process_id in self.state.get_process_ids():
          output = kdb.insert(self.state, process_id)
          logger.debug("kdb insert: %s", output)
  ```

- **Lines 143-148**: Make suggestions during displacement generation
  ```python
  if self.config.kdb_on:
      displacement, mode = kdb.make_suggestion()
      if displacement:
          displacement.mass = self.reactant.mass
          logger.info('Made a KDB suggestion')
          return displacement, mode, 'kdb'
  ```

---

### 4. External Dependencies

The KDB implementation depends on an **external Python package** (not included in eOn):

#### External Package Location
- **Package**: Part of `tsase` Python module
- **URL**: http://theory.cm.utexas.edu/tsase
- **Module name**: `kdb`

#### Required Submodules (imported in eon_kdb.py)
1. **`kdb.aselite`** - File I/O for atomic structures
   - Functions: `read_any()` - reads multiple structure formats
   
2. **`kdb.local_db`** - Database connection and management
   - Class: `LocalDB(dbname)` - opens database file
   - Method: `get_params()` - retrieves KDB parameters dict with keys: `nf`, `dc`, `mac`

3. **`kdb.local_insert`** - Database insertion operations
   - Class: `LocalInsert()`
   - Method: `insert(reactant, saddle, product, mode, nf, dc, mac, kdbname)`

4. **`kdb.local_query`** - Database query operations
   - Class: `LocalQuery()`
   - Method: `query(reactant, output_path, nodupes, kdbname, dc, nf)`

#### Error Handling
- **Lines 15-21** & **Lines 34-40** in `eon_kdb.py`: Bare `except` clauses that silently catch import failures
- If `kdb` module not found: logs error and returns early (graceful degradation)

---

### 5. Configuration Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|---------|
| `use_kdb` | bool | False | Enable/disable KDB entirely |
| `kdb_only` | bool | False | Use ONLY KDB suggestions (no random saddle searches) |
| `remove_duplicates` | bool | False | Prevent duplicate suggestions (slower but prevents redundancy) |
| `kdb_name` | string | "kdb.db" | Database filename |
| `kdb_nf` | float | 0.2 | KDB parameter (undocumented - likely "number fraction" threshold) |
| `kdb_dc` | float | 0.3 | KDB parameter (undocumented - likely "distance cutoff") |
| `kdb_mac` | float | 0.7 | KDB parameter (undocumented - likely "max atom cluster" or "max atom change") |
| `kdb_path` | string | `%(main_directory)s/kdb/` | Database directory path |
| `kdb_scratch_path` | string | `%(main_directory)s/kdbscratch/` | Temporary working directory |

**Undocumented Parameters Issue**: Line 1045 in `schema.py` contains a TODO:
```python
# TODO(rg): These are in config.yaml, not sure what they do..
```
The parameters `kdb_nf`, `kdb_dc`, and `kdb_mac` are passed to KDB functions but lack clear documentation.

---

### 6. Known Issues & Broken/Outdated Aspects

#### A. Bare Exception Handling (eon_kdb.py:19-20, 38-39)
```python
except:
    logger.error('Python module kdb not found, kdb will not be used.')
    return
```
- **Problem**: Uses bare `except:` which catches ALL exceptions (not just ImportError)
- **Impact**: Silently masks unrelated errors in the KDB module
- **Status**: Outdated error handling pattern

#### B. Undocumented KDB Parameters (schema.py:1045)
```python
# TODO(rg): These are in config.yaml, not sure what they do..
kdb_nf: float = 0.2
kdb_dc: float = 0.3
kdb_mac: float = 0.7
```
- **Problem**: Parameters lack clear documentation about their purpose
- **Impact**: Users cannot properly tune KDB behavior
- **Status**: BROKEN/MISSING DOCUMENTATION

#### C. External Dependency Not in Requirements
- **Problem**: `kdb` module is external (from `tsase` package) but not listed in main dependencies
- **Impact**: Installation requires separate setup; graceful degradation means feature silently fails if not installed
- **Status**: Architectural issue

#### D. File-Based Scratch Directory Management (eon_kdb.py:56-74)
```python
dones = glob.glob(os.path.join(config.kdb_scratch_path, "kdbmatches",".done_*"))
# ... manual file reading and deletion
```
- **Problem**: Relies on manual file I/O and glob patterns; no atomic operations
- **Impact**: Race conditions possible in parallel execution; unreliable in network filesystems
- **Status**: Potentially BROKEN in distributed scenarios

#### E. No Input Validation (eon_kdb.py:27-31)
```python
db = local_db.LocalDB(config.kdb_name)
params = db.get_params()
# Uses params['nf'], params['dc'], params['mac'] without validation
```
- **Problem**: No error checking if database is corrupted or params missing
- **Impact**: Silent failures if database corrupted
- **Status**: Robustness issue

#### F. Commented Out Legacy Code (explorer.py:17)
```python
#import kdb
from eon import eon_kdb as kdb
```
- **Problem**: Old direct import is commented out, replaced with wrapper
- **Impact**: Historical artifact suggesting previous refactoring
- **Status**: Minor code cleanliness issue

#### G. Generic Success/Failure Logging (explorer.py:134)
```python
output = kdb.insert(self.state, process_id)
logger.debug("kdb insert: %s", output)
```
- **Problem**: `insert()` function returns `None` (implicit), but logs output anyway
- **Impact**: Useless log entry; no way to detect insertion failures
- **Status**: Logging/diagnostics issue

---

### 7. Test Configuration

**File: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/tests/canary/kdb/config.ini`**
```ini
[KDB]
use_kdb = True
kdb_only = False
remove_duplicates = True
```

**Test file**: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/tests/canary/kdb/test.py`
- Runs 15 AKMC iterations with KDB enabled
- Compares dynamics output with reference file (`dynamics.test`)
- Currently passing (as of last test run)

---

### 8. Historical Development

**Key Git commits** (from `git log --all --grep="kdb"`):
- `ae2746b7`: DOC: Port kdb
- `2dec0d94`, `37a3ae5c`: global kdb and license
- `1a86554c`, `c957b9e9`: removed kdb from inside of eon
- `06132d24`, `3cee25e7`: kdb import and some print -> write statements
- `3c589085`, `0e7893b8`: fix to import kdb
- `4f94879e`, `9bfcd6f5`: eon specific kdb
- `a0855ab9`, `877e1975`: fix kdb bug
- `bd83e6d4`: AKMC simulations now work with new kdb
- `655beeb8`: linking kdb svn

**Observation**: History shows KDB was separated from eOn core at some point (commit `1a86554c/c957b9e9`), suggesting it was made into an external dependency deliberately.

---

### 9. Documentation

**User Guide**: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/docs/source/user_guide/kdb.md`
- References cite: `kdb-terrellDatabaseAtomisticReaction2012`
- Explains use case: stores reactant/saddle/product for local atomic environments
- Notes: requires separate `tsase` package installation
- Provides visual examples from carboxyl group chemistry

**API Documentation**: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/docs/source/apidocs/eon/eon.eon_kdb.md`
- Auto-generated from docstrings
- Currently has minimal docstring content

---

### 10. Summary Table of Issues

| Issue | Severity | Category | File(s) | Lines |
|-------|----------|----------|---------|-------|
| Bare exception handling | Medium | Error Handling | eon_kdb.py | 19-20, 38-39 |
| Undocumented KDB params | High | Documentation | schema.py | 1045 |
| No params validation | Medium | Robustness | eon_kdb.py | 27-31 |
| File-based IPC unreliable | High | Architecture | eon_kdb.py | 56-74 |
| External dependency not tracked | Medium | Dependencies | - | - |
| Useless insert() return logging | Low | Diagnostics | explorer.py | 134 |
| Commented legacy import | Low | Code Quality | explorer.py | 17 |
| No output from make_suggestion errors | Medium | Diagnostics | eon_kdb.py | 56-74 |

---

### 11. File Paths Summary

- **Main KDB wrapper**: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/eon_kdb.py`
- **Configuration schema**: `/home/rgoswami/Git/Github/TheochemUI/eOn/eon/schema.py` (lines 1032-1048)
